extra.reason: config.py, auth.py, dispatcharr_client.py, routes.py, main.py, requirements.txt written -- config.py trimmed to VOD-scoped fields, routes.py trimmed to auth+settings endpoints only, main.py carries the two VOD background tasks verbatim.

All 11 business logic files copied verbatim. Verified: py_compile passes, and every cross-module import (config.py/routes.py/dispatcharr_client.py/auth.py symbols) matches what the new minimal shell actually exports.

package.json (trimmed, no hls.js/mpegts.js), vite/tailwind/postcss/tsconfig configs, index.html, main.tsx, index.css (theme vars, confidence-badge CSS dropped as unused), lib/api.ts (vodmanager-session key), lib/utils.ts, components/ui/{button,card,input}.tsx (verbatim), Login.tsx (VOD Manager branding), Settings.tsx (trimmed to Dispatcharr conn + credentials, EPG fields dropped), App.tsx (settings-gate -> login-gate -> render, minimal top bar with Settings/Logout, no theme picker) all written. Also added the /settings/disconnect/ endpoint to backend routes.py for parity with Settings.tsx's disconnect button.
VodManager.tsx copied verbatim -- confirmed it only depends on already-ported @/components/ui/{button,card} and @/lib/api, no changes needed.

Dockerfile (multi-stage, mirrors epgmatcharr's pattern) and docker-compose.yml (port 8282, own named volume vod_manager_data) written and verified: docker build succeeds end-to-end, container starts cleanly on port 8282, and data persists correctly across container restart.

Full end-to-end verification passed: (1) docker build succeeds, (2) container starts on 8282 with both background tasks (catalog refresher, enrichment scheduler) confirmed scheduled and running via logs, (3) first-run flow verified (Settings page shows when unconfigured, credentials setup, login all work through the real browser UI), (4) added a provider + created a movie + added a source through the full UI stack -- all API calls succeed, (5) streaming code path (xc_server.py) executes cleanly (clean 401 for invalid creds, no crashes) -- full playback verification requires a real Dispatcharr M3U account (by design, same security gate as production), which wasn't available in this test environment but the code is byte-identical to the already-verified EPGmatcharr version, (6) data persists correctly across container restart via the named volume. Also cleaned up leftover EPGmatcharr branding (logger names, Plex/Emby client identifiers, one user-visible settings string) to VOD Manager.

VOD Manager successfully extracted into this standalone project. Backend (config/auth/routes/main.py + all 11 VOD business logic files), frontend (new Vite app scaffold + VodManager.tsx as main page), and Docker/docker-compose (port 8282) all built and verified working end-to-end.
Implemented and verified working end-to-end (download/restore/reset for all 3 components, live-tested against real data).

Built and verified live: _transcode_vod_stream in xc_server.py re-encodes to H.264/AAC on the fly for movie-source/series-source preview paths, confirmed working against a real Plex-sourced .avi file. Whole-file transcode (not HLS), on-demand (starts when Play is clicked, not pre-transcoded), scoped to the in-app preview endpoints only, not the public XC catalog stream endpoints (matches the original scope questions in this issue). Real seek support (HLS segmenting) remains open as vod_manager-1qk -- a jump-to-timestamp partial fix (fresh stream at a chosen offset via ffmpeg -ss, not mid-stream scrubbing) shipped instead as a cheaper stopgap for the actual workflow need (skip past an intro to verify a title).

Fully resolved. The single-instance coordination mechanism (_has_capacity/_live_viewer_count) turned out to already exist in the code but was unconfigured for any real provider -- fixed by configuring it for all 6 real IPTV providers with their actual subscription limits (ProviderA 18, ProviderB 20, ProviderC 5, ProviderD 7, ProviderE 4, ProviderF 6). The multi-instance gap (only checking one hardcoded Dispatcharr account) was the same root cause as vod_manager-e2m -- both fixed together via the new dispatcharr_connections/provider_live_accounts model: _has_capacity now sums live viewer counts across every linked live account on every connected Dispatcharr instance before comparing to a provider's real total limit. Verified live: a real stream request against ProviderB correctly queried its linked live-TV account's viewer count before proceeding.

Fixed together with vod_manager-hgl (same root cause: VOD Manager assumed exactly one Dispatcharr instance). New dispatcharr_connections table generalizes the single implicit connection into a real list; vod_sync.sync_provider now pushes each provider's max_streams to every connection with a vod_relay_account_id configured, not just one. Each connection gets its own separate Dispatcharr profile object (provider_sync_profiles, per-connection since profile ids aren't shared across separate Dispatcharr databases). Also added connect_dispatcharr_instance to automate adding a new instance end-to-end (creates the XC client + Dispatcharr-side M3U account + saved connection in one call) instead of the manual multi-step process this previously required. Verified live: migration correctly carried over the existing connection, all 8 providers sync cleanly to it.

Base integration confirmed solid and extensively exercised throughout the 2026-07-19/20 session: real Plex movies/series playing (including the transcoded fallback for a Plex-sourced .avi file, verified live with jump-to-timestamp), Activity-panel session tracking with Plex Now-Playing heartbeat reporting, real import/sync working. No concrete remaining polish item was ever specified beyond vague placeholders ('richer library-selection UI, transcode-profile tuning') -- closing per the issue's own note to close once the base integration is considered done. Reopen with a specific, concrete ask if a real gap surfaces.

Already fully built (checkboxes on each row, 'Place selected (N)' button, plus a 'place all filtered' variant for the whole-result-set case, both for Movies and TV Shows) -- matches the exact ask (search, select some/all, bulk-place into a category). Verified live end-to-end tonight: created a throwaway test category, bulk-placed 3 real movies into it via the actual API (POST /api/vod/movies/bulk-place/), confirmed all 3 landed, cleaned up. No further work needed.

Done: (1) README.md now documents TLS/reverse-proxy/VPN deployment guidance under Security and deployment. (2) Lockout thresholds (max attempts, window, duration) are now configurable via Settings -> Security, backed by config.py's get/save_lockout_settings() and a new GET/POST /api/vod/lockout-settings/ route; xc_server.py reads them through a 30s-cached _lockout_settings() instead of hardcoded constants. Verified live: lowered threshold to 3 attempts, confirmed a real lockout log line and a 4th request rejected as locked out, then restored defaults. (3) Admin visibility for locked-out IPs left as-is (container logs only) -- issue itself flagged this low priority. (4) In-memory/per-process lockout state left as-is -- issue itself judged this an acceptable trade-off.

Already fully built and confirmed working in production: TMDB Lists integration (config.py TMDB key, tmdb_sync.py fetch_list_items/sync_category/sync_all with exact tmdb_id matching, vod_routes.py sync-source/sync-now endpoints, VodManager.tsx 'TMDB Lists' card with Add-list form and per-category manual sync). This was the issue's own top-ranked recommendation ('start with TMDB Lists specifically -- sidesteps the hardest part'). Verified live against the user's real 'Steven's Movie Picks' list (TMDB list REDACTED_LIST_ID) via browser click -> Sync now -> log confirms match against the real pool. Corrected a stale docstring in tmdb_sync.py claiming a scheduled task already calls sync_all() (it doesn't yet -- tracked separately as vod_manager-tq3). Trakt/Simkl/MyAnimeList integrations from the original title were research options, not requirements -- not pursued since TMDB Lists alone satisfies the use case; can reopen a fresh issue if a non-TMDB source is specifically wanted later.

Built: per-provider-type configurable catalog refresh interval (XC/Plex/Emby/Jellyfin each independently tunable, addressing the original 18+ min Plex-scan-on-shared-6h-interval motivation), configurable enrichment TTL, and a new opt-in TMDB Lists auto-sync scheduler (previously manual-only). Backend: config.py get/save_refresh_settings(), vod_db.py new providers.last_catalog_refresh_at column + cached _refresh_settings()/get_enrichment_ttl_seconds()/get_catalog_refresh_interval_seconds()/mark_provider_catalog_refreshed(), main.py's _vod_catalog_refresher rewritten to poll every 5min and refresh only providers actually due per their type's interval (was: one shared 6h sleep for everything), new _tmdb_sync_scheduler task (disabled by default). API: GET/POST /api/vod/refresh-settings/. Frontend: new 'Refresh Schedule' Settings card (hours-based inputs, seconds under the hood). Verified live: py_compile + tsc clean, Docker rebuild, migration confirmed (last_catalog_refresh_at column present, NULL on existing rows), first-boot correctly flagged all 8 active providers as due (matching old always-refresh-on-fresh-state behavior), confirmed a provider (ProviderA) got stamped with a real timestamp after its refresh completed, POST/GET round-tripped custom values, browser-verified the Settings UI renders and reflects saved values, then restored sane defaults (6h all types, 24h enrichment, TMDB sync off).
